payload.planText: # Plan: Uniform 3:4 Portfolio Grid Refactor ## Section 1 — Non-Functional Requirements - Performance: keep loading="lazy" + decoding="async" on every thumbnail; no layout shift after load (fixed aspect-ratio 3/4 reserves space); no new dependencies (pure CSS grid + existing ). - Usability/Accessibility: preserve all lightbox interactions — click-to-open, Esc, ArrowLeft/Right, prev/next buttons, touch swipe; preserve alt text; do not regress keyboard reachability. - Responsiveness: uniform column count across all sections and both pages — 2 cols mobile, 3 at md, 4 at lg+. Identical gap to today: clamp(6px,0.8vw,12px). - Visual consistency: every thumbnail an equal 3:4 portrait box, center-cropped via object-fit: cover, regardless of source orientation; rows align (uniform grid, not masonry). - Reliability: lightbox still resolves clicked photo to correct index in flat photos array; prev/next bounds unchanged. ## Section 2 — Success Metrics - Functional: both /foodphotography and /propertyphotography render uniform 3:4 grids; clicking any tile opens full UNCROPPED image (object-fit: contain) with working prev/next/Esc/swipe. - Quality: build/typecheck passes; zero references to buildColumns, GalleryLayout, columnLayout, photoOverrides, forcedRatio; galleryColumns.ts deleted with no dangling imports. - Visual: manual check at mobile/tablet/desktop — equal tiles, aligned rows, no cropping in lightbox. ## Section 3 — Risks and Assumptions Assumptions - Tailwind responsive utilities (grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4) available — confirmed by existing className="hidden md:flex" in GalleryGrid.tsx. - GalleryGrid consumed only by the two portfolio pages — verify at build time (grep hook-blocked during planning). - galleryColumns.ts imported only by app/foodphotography/page.tsx. Risk if wrong: deleting breaks another importer. Mitigation: grep for galleryColumns and buildColumns before deleting; build must pass. Risks - Center-cropping landscape sources to 3:4 cuts left/right edges. Mitigation: accepted by user (uncropped view in lightbox); center anchor default. - Property page bespoke count-based buildColumns and the 2752/1536 override removed — confirm no section visually depends on it. Mitigation: uniform grid is the explicit goal; visual review covers it. - Three Photo type definitions exist (galleryColumns.ts, GalleryGrid.tsx, inline property page). Mitigation: consolidate to the GalleryGrid export. ## Section 3.5 — Context Package - app/components/GalleryGrid.tsx — rendering + lightbox; exports Photo. - app/foodphotography/page.tsx — imports buildColumns/Photo/GalleryLayout; per-restaurant layout configs. - app/foodphotography/galleryColumns.ts — module to delete. - app/propertyphotography/page.tsx — local buildColumns (lines 72-107) + inline Photo/Property. - Confirm Tailwind wired (global CSS / config) supporting responsive grid-cols-* classes. ## Section 4 — Tasks - [ ] Refactor GalleryGrid to a uniform 3:4 responsive grid rendered from a flat photos array - Given GalleryGrid currently takes columns: Photo[][] + photos and renders flexbox columns at natural ratios When I drop the columns prop and render a CSS grid (className="grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4", inline style gap clamp(6px,0.8vw,12px)) where each tile is a clickable box with inline aspectRatio "3 / 4", overflow hidden, cursor pointer, and an at width/height 100%, objectFit cover, loading="lazy", decoding="async" Then thumbnails are uniform 3:4 center-cropped tiles and the lightbox block (overlay, prev/next, Esc, swipe) is unchanged and still indexes into photos - NFRs: See Section 1. Remove forcedRatio from the exported Photo interface; keep Photo exported. - Verification Gate: Component compiles; lightbox open/close/prev/next/Esc/swipe still work; no reference to columns prop or forcedRatio remains in the file. - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks - [ ] Convert app/foodphotography/page.tsx to pass flat photos and drop masonry config - Given the page imports buildColumns/Photo/GalleryLayout from ./galleryColumns and defines per-restaurant layout objects When I remove those imports/layout fields, import Photo from ../components/GalleryGrid, and change render to Then each restaurant renders as a uniform 3:4 grid with no leftover layout/forcedRatio/buildColumns references - NFRs: See Section 1. Preserve page chrome (header, back link, per-section labels, rules, spacing). - Verification Gate: Page compiles; food page renders uniform grid in browser; no galleryColumns import remains. - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks - [ ] Convert app/propertyphotography/page.tsx to pass flat photos and delete local buildColumns - Given the page has an inline buildColumns (lines 72-107, incl the 2752/1536 override) and an inline Photo interface When I delete buildColumns, import Photo from ../components/GalleryGrid, drop the inline Photo, and change render to Then each property renders as a uniform 3:4 grid identical in column behavior to the food page - NFRs: See Section 1. Preserve page chrome and per-property labels. - Verification Gate: Page compiles; property page renders uniform grid in browser; no inline buildColumns/forcedRatio remains. - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks - [ ] Delete app/foodphotography/galleryColumns.ts and confirm no dangling references - Given the two pages no longer import from it When I grep the repo for galleryColumns, buildColumns, GalleryLayout, columnLayout, photoOverrides, and forcedRatio and find no remaining references, then delete the file Then the module is gone and the project builds clean - NFRs: Zero dangling imports; build/typecheck passes. - Verification Gate: Grep returns no hits outside git history; build/typecheck succeeds. - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks - [ ] Manual browser verification across breakpoints - Given all code changes are committed When I run the dev server and view both pages at mobile (
